purpose: Governed registry of PMAIOS product methods before they are fully routed into Product Chief and workflow execution.

categories.name: Product Definition
categories.description: Methods used to define what the product is, why it exists, and where the boundary sits.

categories.name: User Research
categories.description: Methods used to clarify users, stakeholders, jobs-to-be-done, pains, and usage context.

categories.name: Requirement Decision
categories.description: Methods used to classify, compare, and accept/reject requirements.

categories.name: Prioritization
categories.description: Methods used to sequence scope, rank opportunity, and cut release scope.

categories.name: Value And Metrics
categories.description: Methods used to define value hypotheses and measurable product success.

categories.name: Solution And Architecture
categories.description: Methods used to translate product intent into capability, boundary, and interaction structure.

categories.name: Retrospective And Learning
categories.description: Methods used to turn outcomes, defects, and drift into future product-process upgrades.

methods.name: 5W2H
methods.problem: Ambiguous product idea needs structured framing before solution or PRD work.
useWhen: goal is known but scope is unclear
product definition must be clarified before delivery

methods.name: Persona
methods.problem: User target is fuzzy, stakeholder expectations conflict, or scenario assumptions are missing.

methods.name: Jobs-To-Be-Done
methods.problem: Feature request exists, but the underlying user progress and switching pressure are not understood.
useWhen: need to distinguish task from desired outcome

methods.name: Kano Model
methods.problem: Requirement set is large, but the team cannot distinguish hygiene items from delight items.
useWhen: release scope must protect basics first

methods.name: RICE
methods.problem: Competing opportunities exist but prioritization is still qualitative and unstable.
useWhen: multiple candidate items must be ranked

methods.name: North Star Metric
methods.problem: Value is discussed abstractly and the product lacks one governing success measure.
useWhen: strategy needs one leading value metric
teams optimize local metrics without common direction
need to connect product work to measurable outcome

methods.name: System Boundary
methods.problem: Solution direction exists, but scope, integration seams, and non-goals are still mixed together.
useWhen: non-goals must be frozen before build
